title: ISS-1590 (EXP-256 falsifiability-module Cond 3 "0 / 69" denominator) is already-fixed-by-source — recommend close-no-patch
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — Condition 3 status span at line 1526. No further patch required. The c1 H6 hole "Condition 3 shows "0 / 69" but eg-falsifiability source does not explicitly state the denominator as 69; should use build-time interpolation (counts.total) rather than hardcoded 69" was already satisfied by the original EXP-256 proposal, which used ${counts.total} interpolation in all four condition status spans from the outset (verbatim segment FM1.insert_text: "0 / ${counts.total}"). The c1 reviewer appears to have misread the proposal as having a hardcoded 69; the rendered "0 / 69" in current docs/index.html is the runtime evaluation of ${counts.total} === 69 (the dome’s claimed win count from data/wins.json:counts.total), not a hardcoded literal in the source. EXP-271 remediation (integrated commit ac474a95 "Operator rescue: ship EXP-271 falsifiability-module integration") preserved the ${counts.total} interpolation while migrating the section to ds- prefix and tightening the framing to "0 / N met" for conditions 3+4 and "0 / N" for condition 1 (per ISS-1656 follow-up). Current source confirmed @ commit 4ee7c952 (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1518/1526/1531 all use ${counts.total}; grep -c "0 / 69" on the source returns 0; only the *built* docs/index.html shows the rendered literal because counts.total is a build-time substitution).
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-256-proposal.json (c1 H6, minor) — proposal_audit.status_grounding flagged "0 / 69 for condition 3 imports a denominator from condition 1 that the source does not explicitly state for the Schumann case"; recommended_action: "Should use build-time interpolation (counts.total) rather than hardcoded 69." Already-implemented at proposal time. Cross-reference: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/overview-falsification-EXP-271.c1.json (post-integration review focused on git-canonical / FUSE-vs-git drift, all H0-H2 already-resolved by subsequent integration; no Condition 3 denominator re-flag).
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:38Z; deep-attack class declared by baby on orphan-pickup; analyst-opus override to verification per PROP-025 author honesty — single-source verification of an interpolation-already-present claim, no adversarial argumentation, batchable).
class_hint_honored: baby-escalated review_class=deep-attack OVERRIDDEN to verification on EXP. Override rationale per PROP-025 author honesty: the close-no-patch determination required only mechanical verification (read EXP-256 segment FM1 for original interpolation, grep current generate-html.js for the same interpolation pattern, confirm test.js EXP-271 block tests the rendered output, confirm git history shows ac474a95 EXP-271 integration preserved interpolation). No new arguments introduced; no defender-pivot; curmudgeon at re-review only needs to confirm the close-no-patch trace lands cleanly. ISS-1590.class_hint=null per PROP-027 (decider M1 sweep severity=minor age>7d default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST).
review_class_rationale: Verification-class because (a) the recommended fix (use ${counts.total} interpolation) was already in the source from EXP-256 proposal segment FM1 onward — verified by direct grep of monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-256-overview-falsification-module.json patch_segments[0].insert_text; (b) the current state preserves it — verified by grep of build-scripts/generate-html.js:1518/1526/1531 (all three condition status spans use ${counts.total}); (c) test.js EXP-271 block (lines 712-746) explicitly encodes the rendering expectations including 2 "0 / N met" spans (conditions 3, 4) per ISS-1656 framing fix; (d) no adversarial work needed — the curmudgeon’s c1 H6 appears to have misread the proposal text as containing a hardcoded 69 when the proposal in fact used interpolation. Counter-argument considered + rejected: one could declare deep-attack because cross-checking three files (proposal/source/test) is non-trivial, but PROP-025 explicitly warns marking refinement work as deep-attack wastes attention budget; the verification work here is exactly the batchable pattern Step 8a is designed to consume.
summary_for_decider: ISS-1590 (minor, EXP-256 c1 H6 "Condition 3 0 / 69 denominator hardcode") is already-fixed-by-source. Resolution kind: already-fixed-by-source (the curmudgeon’s recommended patch was in the original proposal). Audit trail: 1. Original EXP-256 proposal (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-256-overview-falsification-module.json patch_segments[0]) used ${counts.total} interpolation in all four condition status spans, NOT hardcoded 69. Verbatim FM1.insert_text fragment: 0 / ${counts.total}. 2. Current build-scripts/generate-html.js @ commit 4ee7c952 lines 1518, 1526, 1531 all use ${counts.total} (verified by grep). Only the *rendered* docs/index.html shows literal "0 / 69" because counts.total evaluates to 69 (dome’s claimed win count) at build time. 3. EXP-271 remediation (commit ac474a95) preserved the interpolation and added disambiguating sub-statuses ("2 attempted · 0 succeed", "1 attempted · imports globe radius", "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged"). EXP-271 review (overview-falsification-EXP-271.c1.json) raised three holes (git-canonical drift, FUSE rebuild lag, test count mismatch) — all already-resolved by subsequent integration; none re-flag the Condition 3 denominator. 4. test.js lines 712-746 explicitly assert the interpolation rendering: assertEq(zeroNumCount, 2, "EXP-271 updated ISS-1656: exactly 2 "0 / N met" spans (conditions 3, 4); condition 1 now shows "0 / N" per ISS-1656 framing fix"). The interpolation is test-locked. 5. Adjacent concerns (denominator semantics, attempt-vs-fail conflation) are tracked in ISS-1653 and ISS-1656 respectively, both already consolidated into baby-EXP-385/-386 (status: consolidated-into-EXP-385/EXP-386 in expansion-tracker.json). ISS-1590 does NOT need to absorb their scope; it can close as already-fixed-by-source on the literal H6 recommendation. 6. The c1 reviewer’s hole appears to have misread the EXP-256 proposal as having a hardcoded literal 69. The proposal in fact used interpolation from the outset; the "hardcoded" language in the H6 rationale was likely chasing the rendered output rather than the source. Recommended decider action: close ISS-1590 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source, fixed_by_exp=EXP-256 (interpolation present from proposal), fixed_by_exp_secondary=EXP-271 (preserved + tightened framing). No build required, no test changes required.
kernel_of_truth: The c1 H6 reviewer was correct that a hardcoded denominator in this position would be a problem — denominators that look like they were imported from an unattested source DO weaken a critical-review surface. The kernel is real: "where does this number come from?" is the right question for any rendered count. What the reviewer missed is that the answer is already structural — counts.total is the dome’s claimed win count, sourced from data/wins.json at build time, and the eg-falsifiability source closing paragraph explicitly justifies the "0 / counts.total" framing across all four conditions: "the reason the current scorecard shows zero is not that we constructed an impossible standard — it is that none of the ${counts.total} claims meets even the weakest version of this standard." The interpolation + the source justification together address H6’s concern fully.
tldr_evidence: EXP-256 proposal segment FM1 used ${counts.total} interpolation from the outset; current generate-html.js preserves it (verified by grep at lines 1518, 1526, 1531); EXP-271 remediation tightened framing while keeping interpolation; test.js EXP-271 block test-locks the rendering. The curmudgeon’s recommended fix was already in place when the review was written.
tldr_verdict: Close ISS-1590 as already-fixed-by-source. Adjacent concerns (denominator mismatch, attempt-vs-fail conflation) are tracked separately in ISS-1653/1656 (consolidated into baby-EXP-385/-386). No patch, no test, no rebuild needed.
next_steps_for_decider: Single action: close ISS-1590 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source and fix_details pointing to (a) EXP-256 proposal patch_segments[0].insert_text using ${counts.total} from the outset, (b) current build-scripts/generate-html.js:1518/1526/1531 preserving the interpolation, (c) test.js:712-746 EXP-271 block test-locking the rendering. No queue push for re-review needed (verification-class, batchable in curmudgeon Step 8a — but curmudgeon Step 8a will simply observe the closure, no actual review work since no text changed).
